mnemonics.title: K-Means Clustering
mnemonics.content: K-Means clustering is a type of unsupervised machine learning algorithm used to group similar data points into clusters based on their features.

mnemonics.title: Centroids
mnemonics.content: Centroids are the mean values of the features in each cluster, and they are used to represent the cluster.

mnemonics.title: Elbow Method
mnemonics.content: The elbow method is a method for determining the optimal number of clusters by plotting the sum of squared errors against the number of clusters.

acronyms.usage: K-Means clustering is a type of unsupervised machine learning algorithm used to group similar data points into clusters based on their features.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a set of data points in a high-dimensional space, and a set of centroids that represent the clusters. The goal of K-Means clustering is to assign each data point to the cluster with the closest centroid.

analogies.analogy: K-Means clustering is like grouping a set of people into teams based on their characteristics. Each team has a leader (centroid) that represents the team, and the goal is to assign each person to the team with the leader that is most similar to them.
